body:
Title: CRA Form MB428 and Worksheet MB428 2025 Manitoba resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5007-c/5007-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5007-c/5007-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5007-d/5007-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5007-d/5007-d-25e.pdf

MB428 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 47000
Value: 100000

CRA Form MB428 2025 calculates Manitoba tax for Manitoba residents. Part A calculates Manitoba tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $47,000 and $100,000. The rates are 10.8%, 12.75%, and 17.4%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $5,076.00, and $11,833.50.

Part B calculates Manitoba non-refundable tax credits. Worksheet MB428 line 58040 calculates the basic personal amount: $15,780 when net income is less than $200,000, zero when net income is $400,000 or more, and a proportional reduction between those amounts. Line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $3,728, starts reducing above $27,749, is zero at $52,602 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $9,134 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Lines 58200, 58400, 58440, 58480, 58520, 58560, 58600, 58640, and 61470 feed the Manitoba non-refundable credit subtotal. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $1,728 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 10.8%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 10.8% and line 14 by 17.4%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Manitoba non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Manitoba tax. Line 60 adds Manitoba tax on taxable income and Manitoba tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet MB428 line 61520 calculates the Manitoba dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 0.7835% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 8%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 50%. Lines 64 and 65 subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover from tax and floor negative amounts at zero. Line 66 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 1 minus line 2 by 50%. Line 69 calculates the Manitoba political contribution tax credit: contributions over $2,325 receive $1,000; otherwise the first $400 receives 75%, the next range to $750 receives 50% plus $300, and amounts over $750 receive 33.33% plus $475. Lines 70 to 82 subtract political contribution credit, labour-sponsored funds tax credit line 60800, provincial foreign tax credit, community enterprise development tax credit line 60830, small business venture capital tax credit line 60850 with a $120,000 maximum, employee share purchase tax credit line 60860, and mineral exploration tax credit line 60920. Line 82 enters Manitoba tax on line 42800 of the T1 return. Normalized numeric values encoded from MB428 and Worksheet MB428 include 47000, 100000, 0.108, 0.1275, 0.174, 5076, 11833.50, 15780, 200000, 400000, 3728, 27749, 52602, 0.15, 9134, 1728, 0.03, 0.50, 0.007835, 0.08, 400, 750, 2325, 1000, 0.75, 0.3333, 300, 475, and 120000.

heading: CRA Form MB428 and Worksheet MB428 2025 Manitoba Resident Tax
